findings.summary: The CLI never validates the scheme of MCP server URLs — `mcp-servers add` registers any `--url` (create_mcp_server passes it through unchecked) and deploy/bundle only checks registration and can_invoke (mcp_resolver), so a project can reference or register a remote MCP server over cleartext `http://`/`ws://`, violating the remit's hard prohibition that remote MCP URLs MUST use TLS.

Secret redaction on `mcp-servers get` is narrow — `_redact_mcp_server` masks only `headers[].value`, so any other secret-bearing field the platform returns in an MCP server record (e.g. OAuth client secret or token material) would be printed to the terminal in cleartext.

The shipped `libs/cli/THREAT_MODEL.md` documents the retired interactive REPL surface (TUI, local dev server, hooks, sandbox) rather than the current deploy-only CLI, leaving the actual live surface (bundle fidelity, MCP-TLS enforcement, deploy-target confirmation, credential handling) without a current adversarial threat model.
